steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence has a complete independent clause before the blank ("the city of Lüneburg was a member of a powerful mercantile alliance that dominated northern European trade between the 13th and 17th centuries") and a noun phrase after it ("the Hanseatic League, a loose confederation…"). The blank must use punctuation that correctly links an independent clause to a following identification or elaboration.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — colon: a colon after an independent clause correctly introduces an explanation or identification. "The Hanseatic League" identifies the mercantile alliance just named. ✓
  • Choice B — no punctuation: fuses the clause and noun phrase into a run-on. ✗
  • Choice C — period: leaves "the Hanseatic League, a loose confederation…" as a fragment with no main verb. ✗
  • Choice D — semicolon: a semicolon requires an independent clause on both sides; the material after the blank is a noun phrase, not a clause. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
